---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}

```

## Analysis of Waiting Times and Opposite‑Direction Buses Across Peak Periods

 

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)   
#  Read data from URL into data frame 'logged_data'
 
logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQG3iHTYcFJaPBsncTtATK35WDj0nYos1ZubJcnFGp-Tm-vqKYHmCNFLfCmB32nFe2Sx9OOCCS3BrA-/pub?gid=1301837894&single=true&output=csv")

 

 
# 2. Rename variables and create new data frame 'latest_data'
 
latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    timestamp        = Timestamp,
    observation_period = `1. Observation Period`,
    waiting_time     = `2. Waiting Time (minutes)`,
    opposite_buses   = `3.Number of Opposite‑Direction 70 Buses Observed`
  )

 
#   Average waiting time
#    Maximum waiting time
 

mean_wait <- mean(latest_data$waiting_time, na.rm = TRUE)
max_wait <- max(latest_data$waiting_time, na.rm = TRUE)
 

```

### Dynamic Comments

Based on the collected data, the **average waiting time** across all observations is `r round(mean_wait, 1)` minutes.  
The maximum waiting time recorded was `r max_wait` minutes.


### Bar Charts

```{r}
# Chart 1: Waiting time frequency by period
p1 <- ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = factor(waiting_time), fill = observation_period)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Frequency of waiting times by observation period",
       x = "Waiting time (minutes)", y = "Count", fill = "Observation period")  

# Chart 2: Proportion of opposite-direction buses by period
p2 <- ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = observation_period, fill = opposite_buses)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Proportion of opposite-direction buses by period",
       x = "Observation period", y = "Proportion", fill = "Number of buses")  

# Display charts
p1
p2
```
